skill.name: azure-functions
skill.description: Guides Azure Functions development with patterns for isolated worker projects, Durable Functions, language models, cold starts, and production readiness.
skill.summary: Apply production-focused Azure Functions patterns across .NET, Node.js, Python, and Durable Functions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a high-severity weak cryptography pattern at SKILL.md:3. Manual review found that line is only descriptive metadata for Azure Functions and contains no cryptographic algorithm, executable code, external calls, or prompt injection attempt. No additional semantic security issues were found in the single scanned file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 for weak cryptography, but the line only describes Azure Functions topics. No weak cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain frontmatter text and contains no code or cryptographic references. The full file contains only guidance headings and no executable behavior.

content.value_statement: Azure Functions projects often fail from outdated models, blocking calls, and poor runtime configuration. This skill gives concise patterns for modern serverless functions, Durable Functions, and production readiness.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes modern Azure Functions programming models for .NET, Node.js, and Python.
Highlights Durable Functions patterns for orchestration and async workflows.
Identifies common anti-patterns such as blocking async calls and per-request HTTP clients.
Calls out operational sharp edges including timeouts, extension bundles, and Application Insights.
Supports production planning for cold start mitigation and isolated worker adoption.
limitations: Provides guidance only and does not generate deployable project files by itself.
Does not replace current Azure documentation for service limits or pricing.
Does not inspect a live Azure subscription or deployed Function App.
Contains concise pattern notes, not full tutorials for every runtime.

use_cases.title: Modernize Function Apps
use_cases.description: Review an existing Azure Functions plan and identify outdated programming models or runtime choices.

use_cases.title: Design Durable Workflows
use_cases.description: Plan async orchestration patterns with Durable Functions before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Reviews
use_cases.description: Check serverless designs for cold starts, telemetry, timeout limits, and dependency handling.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Runtime Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new function app
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a new Azure Functions app. Compare the .NET isolated worker, Node.js v4, and Python v2 models for my use case.

prompt_templates.title: Review Function Anti-Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure Functions design for blocking async calls, HTTP client misuse, timeout risks, and outdated runtime patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Durable Functions Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Durable Functions orchestration for a long-running workflow. Include activity boundaries, retry behavior, and timeout concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Production Readiness Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production readiness review for my Azure Function App covering cold starts, telemetry, extension bundles, async patterns, and hosting constraints.

output_examples.input: Review a new payment processing Function App design.
output: Recommends an isolated worker model for new .NET projects.
Flags blocking calls and per-request HTTP clients as launch risks.
Suggests telemetry and timeout checks before release.

output_examples.input: Plan a long-running document processing workflow.
output: Frames the work as a Durable Functions orchestration.
Separates trigger, orchestration, and activity responsibilities.
Adds retry and timeout considerations for production behavior.
Checks cold start exposure and hosting plan constraints.
Reviews extension bundle and dependency initialization concerns.

best_practices: Use isolated worker for new .NET Azure Functions projects.
Prefer async and reusable HTTP client patterns for scalable execution.
Validate timeout, telemetry, and extension bundle settings before production release.
anti_patterns: Do not use blocking async calls inside function handlers.
Do not create a new HTTP client for every request.
Do not start new projects on the in-process .NET model without a strong reason.

faq.question: Does this skill create Azure Functions code?
faq.answer: It provides architecture and implementation guidance. You can ask your AI tool to apply the guidance to your codebase.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers .NET isolated worker, Node.js v4, and Python v2 programming models.

faq.question: Does it support Durable Functions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for orchestration and async workflow patterns.

faq.question: Can it inspect my Azure subscription?
faq.answer: No. It does not connect to Azure or inspect live resources.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Manual review found no executable code, network behavior, or prompt injection. The static cryptography finding was dismissed.

faq.question: What should I verify against official Azure docs?
faq.answer: Check current runtime support, hosting limits, timeout rules, pricing, and extension bundle versions before release.
